# launchdarkly_sdk_key (Data Source)

Provides a LaunchDarkly SDK key data source.

~> **Beta:** This data source uses a beta API. Beta resources may change or be removed in future versions.

This data source allows you to retrieve information about a specific SDK key in a project environment.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "launchdarkly_sdk_key" "mobile_analytics" {
  project_key     = "example-project"
  environment_key = "production"
  key             = "mobile-analytics-key"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `environment_key` (String) The environment key.
- `key` (String) The user-defined identifying key of the SDK key.
- `project_key` (String) The project key.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) The description of the SDK key.
- `expiry` (Number) The expiration date for the SDK key, expressed as a Unix epoch time in milliseconds, if set.
- `id` (String) The unique resource ID in the format `project_key/environment_key/key`.
- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this SDK key is the system-defined default for the environment.
- `kind` (String) The kind of SDK key. Either `sdk` (server-side) or `mobile`.
- `name` (String) The human-readable name of the SDK key.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) The actual SDK key value. Use this when configuring your SDK.
- `version` (Number) The auto-incremented version number of the SDK key.
